description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a broad, flat top surface and a solid, geometric base. The sides of the block feature subtle textural variations, with one face displaying a circular indentation and another face showing a rectangular recess, suggesting some decorative or functional detailing. The material appears to be a polished or semi-polished stone, possibly granite or marble, with a slightly reflective, smooth finish. The block is robust and heavy, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment such as a plaza, sidewalk, or park. Its design is modern and monolithic, with clean lines and a sense of permanence. There are no visible openings, engravings, or movable parts, emphasizing its function as a static, supportive structure.
description: A large, polished stone block with a flat top and geometric base, featuring subtle decorative indentations. Its smooth, reflective surface and solid build make it suitable as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Narrow end view showing the block's width and a series of vertical grooves or slots on the base.
description_view_1: Wide face with a prominent circular indentation centered on the base, highlighting decorative detailing.
description_view_2: Opposite narrow end view, mirroring view 0 with vertical grooves or slots visible on the base.
description_view_3: Wide face with a rectangular recess on the base, providing a contrasting detail to the circular indentation on the opposite side.
attribute_location_context: urban plaza or sidewalk as a protective or decorative barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a leveled foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with minor surface irregularities.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely placed as a standalone barrier or obstacle
